title: Key to Genera of Carabidae in Indiana

lead_a.text: Head and pronotum narrowed and elongate, the latter much longer than wide; mandibles slender and elongate, modified for entering gastropod shells.

lead_b.text: Head and pronotum not elongate-narrowed; mandibles of typical predaceous form.

lead_a.text: Large (20+ mm); body robust; pronotum strongly cordate with broadly explanate margins; elytra usually metallic or with vivid colors.

lead_b.text: Variable in size, often <20 mm; pronotum not as above, or coloration uniformly dark.

lead_a.text: Elytra brilliantly metallic (green, blue, or coppery), with regular striae; antennal segments 5–11 pale; readily flies.

lead_b.text: Elytra non-metallic or only weakly so, often with raised costae; antennae uniformly piceous; flightless.

lead_a.text: Body uniformly deep black, robust; mandibles sickle-shaped and asymmetric; pronotum widest near anterior third; tarsi simple.

lead_b.text: Body color and form variable; mandibles symmetrical; pronotum widest at middle or posteriorly.

genus_keys.title: Key to Species of Calosoma in Indiana

lead_a.text: Body 25–35 mm; pronotum and head metallic blue-violet, elytra brilliant green with red or coppery marginal reflections.

lead_b.text: Body smaller, or coloration not as above.

lead_a.text: Body 21–28 mm; elytra uniformly bright green without marginal red; native to Europe; uncommon, established near urban areas.

lead_b.text: Elytra black or dull bronze, not bright green.

lead_a.text: Elytra dull bronze-black with three rows of small golden ocellate punctures on each.

lead_b.text: Elytra uniformly deep black, glossy, lacking distinct golden punctures.
